structured.summary: The paper introduces Proto-Trex networks, a novel approach to enhance the interpretability of transformer language models by integrating prototype networks into their architecture, allowing for interactive learning and user feedback to improve model explanations.

strengths.point: The paper proposes a novel integration of prototype networks into transformer architectures to enhance interpretability.
The approach allows for interactive learning, enabling users to provide feedback and improve model explanations.
Proto-Trex networks perform on par with non-interpretable baselines, demonstrating competitive performance.

weaknesses.point: The paper lacks detailed experimental results and quantitative analysis of the interpretability improvements.
The scalability of the proposed method to large-scale datasets and real-world applications is not discussed.
The paper does not provide a comprehensive comparison with other state-of-the-art interpretability methods.
The interactive learning setup's practical implementation and user experience are not thoroughly explored.
The paper could benefit from more clarity in explaining the prototype learning process and its integration with transformers.

questions.question: How does the performance of Proto-Trex networks compare quantitatively to other interpretability methods in terms of user trust and understanding?
questions.motivation: Understanding the comparative advantage of Proto-Trex networks in real-world applications is crucial for assessing their practical utility.

questions.question: What are the computational costs associated with the interactive learning setup, and how do they affect the model's scalability?
questions.motivation: Evaluating the computational efficiency is important for determining the feasibility of deploying the model in large-scale applications.

questions.question: Can the authors provide more detailed examples of how user feedback is incorporated into the model and its impact on model performance?
questions.motivation: Clarifying the feedback mechanism and its effects would help in understanding the practical implementation and benefits of the interactive learning approach.

critique_points.point_001: The paper lacks detailed experimental results and quantitative analysis of the interpretability improvements. (Abstract and Section 3.2)
critique_points.point_002: The scalability of the proposed method to large-scale datasets and real-world applications is not discussed. (Full text, no specific section)
critique_points.point_003: The paper does not provide a comprehensive comparison with other state-of-the-art interpretability methods. (Section 2)
critique_points.point_004: The interactive learning setup's practical implementation and user experience are not thoroughly explored. (Abstract and Section 2)
critique_points.point_005: The paper could benefit from more clarity in explaining the prototype learning process and its integration with transformers. (Section 3.1 and 3.2)
critique_points.point_006: How does the performance of Proto-Trex networks compare quantitatively to other interpretability methods in terms of user trust and understanding? (Understanding the comparative advantage of Proto-Trex networks in real-world applications is crucial for assessing their practical utility.)
critique_points.point_007: What are the computational costs associated with the interactive learning setup, and how do they affect the model's scalability? (Evaluating the computational efficiency is important for determining the feasibility of deploying the model in large-scale applications.)
critique_points.point_008: Can the authors provide more detailed examples of how user feedback is incorporated into the model and its impact on model performance? (Clarifying the feedback mechanism and its effects would help in understanding the practical implementation and benefits of the interactive learning approach.)
